subject: Use ONAP recommended base Java Docker image
commit_message:
Use ONAP recommended base Java Docker image

Issue-ID: CPS-304

Signed-off-by: Rishi.Chail <rishi.chail@est.tech>
Change-Id: I71db1d9c85225a33dd9fc4f2e5f7045d71ad3843

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

the task is defined aa bug, but this is an enhancement indeed

Patch Set 1:

Here is an extract of a discussion with Rishi about which registry to retrieve the image from either onap nexus or docker.io:

We also have a pipeline to build internally. It uses Bell internal registry by specifying the maven environment variable . It is currently:

   -Dbase.image=<bell-registry>/openjdk:11.0.9-jre-slim

But it would be cleaner if we could add a new variable `base-image-registry` or something like that. Its default value would be empty. In pom.xml, we could define:

   <base.image>${base-image-registry}onap/integration-java11:8.0.0</base.image>

It would give the option avoid docker.io and use onap nexus if needed, and for our Bell internal pipeline it would ensure that we are still aligned with cps default base image and we are only changing the registry.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Here is an extract of a discussion with Rishi about which registry to retrieve the image from either onap nexus or docker.io:
> 
> We also have a pipeline to build internally. It uses Bell internal registry by specifying the maven environment variable . It is currently:
> 
>    -Dbase.image=<bell-registry>/openjdk:11.0.9-jre-slim
> 
> But it would be cleaner if we could add a new variable `base-image-registry` or something like that. Its default value would be empty. In pom.xml, we could define:
> 
>    <base.image>${base-image-registry}onap/integration-java11:8.0.0</base.image>
> 
> It would give the option avoid docker.io and use onap nexus if needed, and for our Bell internal pipeline it would ensure that we are still aligned with cps default base image and we are only changing the registry.

well approach should be the same as we did for pushing the images. **Note <image>${nexus.repository}**
but in proposed solution will look like <image>${base.image.repo}${base.image}</image>
having base.image.repo defined with empty value by default, as you proposed.

So basically on jib config

                        <from>
                            <image>${base.image}</image>
                        </from>
                        <to>
                            <tags>
                                <tag>latest</tag>
                            </tags>
                            <image>${nexus.repository}${image.name}:${image.version}-${maven.build.timestamp}</image>
                        </to>

Thus, I agree with proposed change, I only will do it in different place just to keep consistency with previous config. At any rate I'm OK with both approaches

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Here is an extract of a discussion with Rishi about which registry to retrieve the image from either onap nexus or docker.io:
> > 
> > We also have a pipeline to build internally. It uses Bell internal registry by specifying the maven environment variable . It is currently:
> > 
> >    -Dbase.image=<bell-registry>/openjdk:11.0.9-jre-slim
> > 
> > But it would be cleaner if we could add a new variable `base-image-registry` or something like that. Its default value would be empty. In pom.xml, we could define:
> > 
> >    <base.image>${base-image-registry}onap/integration-java11:8.0.0</base.image>
> > 
> > It would give the option avoid docker.io and use onap nexus if needed, and for our Bell internal pipeline it would ensure that we are still aligned with cps default base image and we are only changing the registry.
> 
> well approach should be the same as we did for pushing the images. **Note <image>${nexus.repository}**
> but in proposed solution will look like <image>${base.image.repo}${base.image}</image>
> having base.image.repo defined with empty value by default, as you proposed.
> 
> So basically on jib config
> 
>                         <from>
>                             <image>${base.image}</image>
>                         </from>
>                         <to>
>                             <tags>
>                                 <tag>latest</tag>
>                             </tags>
>                             <image>${nexus.repository}${image.name}:${image.version}-${maven.build.timestamp}</image>
>                         </to>
> 
> Thus, I agree with proposed change, I only will do it in different place just to keep consistency with previous config. At any rate I'm OK with both approaches

Both approaches are fine. Having discussed it internally and checking the request limits for docker.io it seems reasonable we always pull Onap specific images from nexus only, as team and builds are ever growing and we may hit limit in future. Even if we don't why to risk it. Thus have hardcoded nexus for pull request. The case for push as is already implemented, is a different one. Also, even with this change, i believe the value can still be overridden for specific env like Bell labs.
